name: Flaxseed Bread

ingredients.item: • 1 cup warm or hot water + 2 1/3 cup + 1 tbsp golden

method: Set the oven to 180 Celsius/360 degrees Fahrenheit.
Make a fine flour out of the flaxseeds. If possible, sift the
flaxseed flour to remove any natural "seeds."
Combine the flaxseed meal, baking powder, baking soda, and
salt in a large mixing basin. Stir in the apple cider vinegar and
water for approximately 30 seconds, or until the flaxseed meal
turns sticky and holds together in a lump. Then, loosely form the
dough into a loaf shape with your hands. (Be careful not to over-
knead the dough.) It should be handled as little as possible). The
surface of the bread should be beautiful and smooth.
Place the bread in a loaf pan that has been oiled (or lined with
parchment paper). A 1-pound loaf pan fits the bread nicely (8.5"
x 4.5"). However, if you have a 1.5-pound loaf pan (10" x 5"),
as I have, it will suffice. (If you're using a bigger loaf pan, shape
the loaf, then set it in the pan without pressing it into the
corners.) Alternatively, a baking sheet coated with parchment
paper can be used. Because the bread retains its shape nicely
after baking, a loaf pan is not required.
Sprinkle some seeds on top and mist with water (I use a mix of
flaxseeds, sesame seeds, and sunflower seeds). To help the
seeds stick better, gently push down on them with your palm.
If you watched the video that accompanies this recipe, you'll see
that I mentioned I let the bread rest for a few minutes, covered.
If you're using baking soda, though, you should do the reverse.
A chemical reaction occurs when baking soda (base) and apple
cider vinegar (acid) are combined. Carbon dioxide is produced
as a byproduct of this process, giving your baked products a
boost. As soon as the primary and acid are combined, the
reaction begins. So, as soon as you combine the two, pop the
bread in the oven before the response cools down.
Set the oven to 350°F and bake the bread for 60 minutes. It's
best if the crust is golden brown (if using golden flaxseeds).
Remove the bread from the oven and cool for 10 minutes in the
loaf pan. Before slicing the bread, place it on a cooling rack to
cool fully.
Refrigerate the flaxseed bread for up to 7 days in an airtight
container. Then refrigerate for up to 3 months in an airtight
container for long-term storage. I prefer to slice the bread before
freezing it, but the whole loaf may be frozen as well.
